stories.title: Skubal Makes Rehab Start Today — Two-Time Cy Young Eyes June 14 Return

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Tarik Skubal is pitching today. High-A West Michigan, less than five weeks after elbow surgery. The two-time Cy Young winner's comeback bid starts now — and if it goes well, he could be back in the Tigers rotation by June 14. ⚾ #DetroitTigers

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Skubal's rehab situation: nano-scope surgery May 6, first live game reps today. Scott Boras says "short rehab." Tigers are 6-21 without him. Every contender has their eye on the trade deadline and his arm. #DetroitTigers #MLB

research.facts:
Skubal Rehab Start — Return Timeline
**Source: MLB.com / ESPN / Last Word On Baseball / ClutchPoints / SI.com**
- Tarik Skubal making rehab start TODAY (June 7, Sunday) with High-A West Michigan Whitecaps vs. Dayton
- Surgery: May 6 — 'nano-scope' procedure to remove loose body from left elbow; new minimally invasive take on arthroscopic surgery (previously took 2-3 months to return, Skubal on track in under 5 weeks)
- Tigers AJ Hinch announced rehab assignment
- High-A chosen over Triple-A Toledo due to weather in Grand Rapids being better than Iowa
- If rehab start goes well, target return: June 14 at Cleveland (next weekend)
- Tigers record since Skubal went on IL around May 4: 6-21 (cited in June 6 pipeline as Tier 2 story)
- Skubal is a two-time Cy Young Award winner (back-to-back)
- He becomes a free agent after the 2026 season — trade deadline August 3 looms
- Scott Boras is Skubal's agent; expects a "short rehab"
**Confidence: HIGH** (MLB.com, ESPN, official team announcements)

Braves 42-21 — Best Record in Baseball
**Source: ESPN / DeepMetricAnalytics / Game7 / CBS Sports**
- Atlanta Braves record: 42-21 as of approximately June 7 standings
- NL East lead: 8.5 games over Philadelphia Phillies (33-29)

- Braves and Dodgers competing for No. 1; Brewers No. 3 (first time all three top teams from NL)
- Ronald Acuña Jr. recovered from thumb bone bruise (X-rays negative earlier, day-to-day); has been hitting well
- Chris Sale anchoring the rotation (from June 6 — "Sale + Riley carrying ATL")
- Matt Olson continuing at 1B
- CBS Sports "Two-Month Grades": Braves are A+ earners
**Confidence: HIGH** (ESPN, standings confirmed multiple sources)

stories.title: Murakami Named AL Rookie of Month for May — Historic 20-HR Pace Even After IL Stint

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Munetaka Murakami wins the AL Rookie of the Month for May — 8 HRs, 18 RBI, 19 walks in 26 games. He has 20 HRs in 55 career games, 4th fastest in MLB history. Currently on IL (hamstring) but the historic pace doesn't pause. #WhiteSox #MLB

research.facts:
Murakami AL Rookie of Month for May
**Source: MLB.com press release / CBS Sports / ESPN / Baseball Reference**
- **Official:** Munetaka Murakami (White Sox) named AL Rookie of the Month for May 2026
- May stats: .244 BA (22-for-90), 8 HRs, 18 RBI, 4 doubles, 19 walks, 21 runs, 1 SB, .382 OBP, .556 SLG in 26 games
- Season: 20 HRs through 55 career games — 4th fastest in MLB history to 20 HRs
- Fastest Japanese-born player to reach 20 MLB HRs (previously Ohtani at 101 games)
- White Sox went from 121 losses in 2024 to Wild Card contenders in 2026
- Grade 2 hamstring IL (placed ~May 31, 2-4 weeks out) — return expected late June / early July
- 5-game HR streak had tied White Sox franchise record (from May 22-23 pipeline entries)
- Age 26; from Japan's NPB (Yakult Swallows); handled April/May MLB adjustment extremely well
- Exit velocity 94.2, hard hit % 58.7, wOBA .400, barrel% 20.7
**Confidence: HIGH** (MLB official press release, multiple sources)

stories.title: AL East Race Tightens — Yankees Lead Rays by Half a Game Without Judge

x_posts.label: Post 4

AL East right now:

research.facts:
AL East Race — Yankees Lead Rays by Half Game Without Judge
**Source: ESPN / MLB.com / Yahoo Sports**
- Yankees 37-25 (first place by 0.5 games)
- Rays 36-23 (second place)
- Blue Jays 30-33, Orioles 30-33 — both 8 GB
- Boston Red Sox 26-35 — 11 GB
- Aaron Judge: diagnosed with stress fracture of right rib (1st rib), placed on 10-day IL June 4
- Recovery: 4-6 weeks, reimaged to determine healing before return — out through All-Star break (mid-July), possibly through August 3 trade deadline
- Spencer Jones recalled from Triple-A to fill roster gap
- José Caballero and Max Schuemann played right field during Judge's initial absence
- Yankees feared season-ending injury — avoiding that is the silver lining
- ESPN: "Time to panic in the Bronx?" — AL East impact explored
**Confidence: HIGH** (MLB.com official, ESPN, Yahoo Sports)

research.facts:
Weekend Roundup
**Source: ESPN / Fox Sports / Search results**
- Saturday June 6: Jacob Misiorowski set velocity records in Brewers win over Rockies 7-1 (from first search result); Misiorowski previously set 103.6 mph all-time starter record on May 27
- Houston Astros won big on June 6 (total scored over 15 per betting lines)
- Toronto won with total over 8.5 on June 6
- Ohtani 2026: 0.74 ERA (3rd best in MLB history since 1913 through first 10 starts — deGrom 0.56, Marichal 0.59 only better); also NL OBP leader; two-way season
- Mariners 4-0 over Tigers on June 7 (per first search result)
- Royals vs Twins 1-1 during play (not final)
- Sunday June 7: full schedule
**Confidence: MEDIUM** (Some Saturday results from betting data lines, not box scores)
